title: Right coordinate absolute value is bounded by the two-entry infinity norm

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any two real entries, the absolute value of the right coordinate is at most max(|x|, |y|).
assumptions: x and y are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The right coordinate is bounded by the two-entry infinity norm.

declarations.name: vec2InfNorm
declarations.statement: def vec2InfNorm (x y : Real) : Real := max |x| |y|

declarations.name: abs_right_le_vec2InfNorm
declarations.statement: theorem abs_right_le_vec2InfNorm (x y : Real) : |y| <= vec2InfNorm x y

tasks.input: Translate the right-coordinate bound for the two-entry infinity norm into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem abs_right_le_vec2InfNorm (x y : Real) : |y| <= vec2InfNorm x y

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding vec2InfNorm and using the right max inequality.
